tagline: The CSS-native, signal-driven, AI-first React design system
authoringRules: Signals only: useSignal, useComputed, useSignalEffect from @cascade-ui/core
No useState, useEffect, useContext, useLayoutEffect, useReducer in components
DOM side effects via useSignalEffect, not useEffect
useRef only for DOM element references
CSS custom properties only — no Tailwind, no inline styles, no CSS-in-JS
Visual states (hover/focus/active) via CSS pseudo-classes — not JS
data-state attributes only for states CSS cannot express (e.g. loading, error)
FSM (useMachine) only when the component itself drives transitions
User-visible strings via @cascade-ui/i18n — no hardcoded English fallbacks
WCAG 2.1 AA minimum — keyboard navigable, screen-reader tested

strict.note: Visual props must resolve to --cascade-* tokens; never hard-code color, spacing, or size values
WCAG 2.1 AA contract is fixed; don't weaken roles, remove aria attributes, or break keyboard interaction
Signals only (useSignal, useComputed, useSignalEffect); no useState/useEffect/useContext/useReducer in cascade components
User-visible component chrome strings must use @cascade-ui/i18n; no hard-coded English fallbacks in component source
All visual values via --cascade-* tokens; no Tailwind, no inline styles, no CSS-in-JS

flexible.note: Choose any step on the --cascade-space-* scale to fit the layout; no single correct answer
User-facing copy (via labels prop) is free within content-tone guidance
Compose cascade components freely; blocks and sections are starting points, not locked layouts
data-theme can be applied to any container, not just :root; themes can be scoped
Which variant to use (primary/secondary/ghost) is a design decision — choose what fits the hierarchy

Accordion.note: type prop is free to choose based on whether sections are exclusive
Borders, surfaces, and radii must resolve to --cascade-* semantic tokens

Alert.note: Choose the severity variant that matches the message; default is neutral

AlertDialog.note: No light-dismiss — closing must go through onConfirm/onCancel
confirm/cancel copy overridable via labels; defaults come from the i18n catalog
Visual styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* tokens; destructive variant uses --cascade-color-destructive

Avatar.note: Pick the size that fits the surrounding density
Fallback and status colors must resolve to --cascade-* semantic tokens

Badge.note: Choose the variant that matches the semantic meaning
Colors and radius must resolve to --cascade-* tokens (--cascade-radius-badge)

Blockquote.note: cite is optional; omit when the source is given in surrounding context
Border and text colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Breadcrumb.note: Collapse long trails to fit available width
Text colors and focus ring must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Button.note: Visual props must resolve to --cascade-button-* / semantic tokens

Card.note: Choose elevation and density to fit the surrounding layout
Surface, border, radius, and shadow must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Checkbox.note: Spreads InputHTMLAttributes — name, value, required, checked, onChange all pass through
Control styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-sm tokens

CheckboxCard.note: Supports both checked + onCheckedChange and defaultChecked

Code.note: sm fits dense UI; md matches body text
Font and surface must resolve to --cascade-font-mono and --cascade-* tokens

Combobox.note: Filtering can be toggled off for short lists via searchable={false}
Listbox/field styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-* tokens

CommandMenu.note: Global Cmd/Ctrl+K binding can be disabled via hotkey={false} for custom triggering
onQueryChange + loading allow fetching items per keystroke
Palette styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-modal tokens

ContextMenu.note: Accepts arbitrary ContextMenuItem children after the trigger child
Anchored at pointer coordinates via --cascade-context-x/y custom properties set on right-click

CopyButton.note: copy/copied strings overridable via labels; default from i18n catalog
Styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* tokens; copied state uses --cascade-color-success

DataTable.note: Choose client/server sort and density to fit data size and layout
All surfaces, borders, and spacing must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

DatePicker.note: All date props are ISO YYYY-MM-DD strings
Display, weekday labels, and week start derive from the current i18n locale via Intl

Dropdown.note: Any ReactElement works as the trigger; ref and aria props are merged in
Supports open + onOpenChange or fully internal state
Menu styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-z-dropdown tokens

Editable.note: Visual styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* surface/border/accent/text tokens
submitOnBlur toggles whether blurring confirms or cancels the edit

EmptyState.note: Action and icon are optional; size scales for full-page vs in-panel use
Text and background colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

FileUploader.note: Status colors must resolve to --cascade-color-accent/success/danger tokens
Overridable via label/hint props or the labels object
Configure freely; rejected files surface through onRejected

Form.note: Use schema, validate, or both; schema runs first and validate only if the schema passes
Any control can be wired via form.field(name); children are free-form
onValid only fires when validation produces no errors; submission is guarded by the submitting signal

Header.note: Brand, links, and actions slots are optional and composable
Surface, border, and z-index must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

HeaderPanel.note: Surface, shadow, and sizing must resolve to --cascade-* shell tokens

Heading.note: size may override the level-derived default for visual scale
Levels must not skip — maintain a valid heading hierarchy

HoverCard.note: Surface, border, radius, shadow, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
openDelay and closeDelay are tunable per instance (defaults 300/100ms)

Input.note: Surface, border, accent, destructive, radius, and focus-ring must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
sm | md | lg, defaulting to md

InputGroup.note: Addon background, border, text, and radius must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
InputGroupAddon align is inline-start (leading) or inline-end (trailing)
prefix/suffix and addon children accept arbitrary ReactNode

Kbd.note: sm fits inline help; md matches body text
Surface and border must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Link.note: standalone vs inline depending on whether the link sits in prose
Accent colors and focus ring must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

List.note: disc/decimal/none chosen to match content and visual needs
Spacing and text color must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Menu.note: Surface, border, radius, shadow, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
MenuItem accepts arbitrary children; onSelect defines the action
MenuItem and MenuSeparator can be mixed freely under the trigger

Modal.note: Overlay surface, border, radius, shadow, and focus-ring must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
sm | md | lg, defaulting to md
All optional; body accepts arbitrary children

MultiSelect.note: Surface, border, accent, radius, shadow, focus-ring, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
placeholder, selected(count), search, and noResults are overridable
Caller supplies the option list and may mark individual options disabled

NumberInput.note: Visual props must resolve to the listed --cascade-* semantic tokens
min, max, step, precision, and formatOptions are free to suit the value domain

OtpInput.note: Slot styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
length and numeric/alphanumeric type are free to match the issued code format

OverflowMenu.note: Trigger and item styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
items, ariaLabel, and placement are free to suit the context

Pagination.note: pageSizeOptions and the size select are optional
Surfaces, borders, and accent must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

PasswordInput.note: Field, toggle, and strength-meter styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
reveal, hide, and strengthLabel can be overridden via the labels prop

Popover.note: Panel styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
Trigger and panel content are arbitrary children supplied by the consumer

ProgressBar.note: Presence of value selects the mode
Fill and status colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

ProgressCircle.note: showValue pairs best with md/lg sizes
Arc and track colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

ProgressIndicator.note: horizontal or vertical to fit the layout
Step and connector colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Prose.note: Accepts authored children or rendered markup
All typography and surface styling resolves to --cascade-* tokens

Radio.note: Control and label styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
Labels are free text and the group supports horizontal or vertical orientation

RadioCard.note: Card and indicator styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
title and description accept arbitrary ReactNode content

RatingGroup.note: Star styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
max sets the scale and labels.rating customizes the per-star announcement

Search.note: Field and control styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
debounceMs and the placeholder/label/clear copy are free to suit the context

SegmentedControl.note: Segment styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
option label and value are free, and individual options may be disabled

Select.note: Visual props must resolve to --cascade-color-* / radius / focus-ring tokens
Free, supplied by the consumer via the options array

Separator.note: Mark decorative when the break carries no meaning beyond visuals

Sheet.note: Limited to start | end | top | bottom — drives the slide direction and animation
Any children; consumer owns the panel contents

ShellHeader.note: Brand, nav, actions, and end are composable and optional
Surfaces, sizing, and accent must resolve to --cascade-* shell tokens

SideNav.note: Rail behavior is configurable for density needs
Sizing, surfaces, and motion must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Skeleton.note: Shape, width, height, and line count match the pending content
Background and radius must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

SkipNav.note: targetId/id may be customized as long as they match
SkipNavLink must be the first focusable element to function

Slider.note: Track and thumb colors must resolve to --cascade-color-* / radius / focus-ring tokens

Spinner.note: sm/md/lg to fit inline vs standalone use

Stat.note: Optional — include only when change is meaningful

Status.note: Enable pulse only for actively-changing states

Switcher.note: Accent and surface colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Tabs.note: Use value or defaultValue depending on control needs
Accent, borders, and focus ring must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Tag.note: onDismiss is optional; variant matches semantic meaning
Variant colors must resolve to --cascade-color-*-subtle tokens

TagsInput.note: Free text, optionally constrained by the validate predicate and max count
Enter and comma commit a tag; Backspace on empty removes the last — fixed interaction contract

Text.note: Choose element and treatment to fit context
Font, leading, and color must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

Textarea.note: Border/focus/error colors must resolve to --cascade-color-* / focus-ring tokens
Consumer chooses initial rows and whether the field can resize

TimePicker.note: Value is a 24-hour HH:mm string driven by the native time input

Toast.note: Limited to default | success | warning | destructive — drives color and live-region urgency

Toggle.note: Track and thumb colors must resolve to --cascade-color-* / radius / focus-ring tokens
Optional label describing the setting being toggled

Tooltip.note: Limited to top | right | bottom | left, positioned via CSS anchor
Consumer can tune the show delay (default 200ms)

VisuallyHidden.note: Any text/content meant for assistive tech
Must keep content in the a11y tree — never display:none

exceptions.why: The `grid-template-rows` trick is the only CSS-only method to animate `height: auto` without JavaScript. It is technically a layout property but uses subgrid containment to avoid reflow costs associated with `height` transitions. The animation audit script explicitly allowlists this component for this reason.

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Vertically stacked, collapsible content sections

whenToUse: Progressively disclosing sections of related content the user reads top to bottom
Reducing vertical scroll when most sections stay collapsed (FAQs, settings groups)
Allowing multiple sections open at once (type="multiple")
whenNotToUse: Switching between mutually exclusive, equally important views — use Tabs
A single show/hide region — a plain disclosure is enough

antiPatterns.bad: <Accordion> with one item that is always open
antiPatterns.good: Render the content directly, or use a collapsible disclosure
antiPatterns.why: A single permanently-open section adds chrome and indirection for no gain

related.name: Tabs
related.reason: Tabs switch between peer views; Accordion stacks sequential sections vertically

intent.a11yRationale: Each trigger is a native <button> exposing aria-expanded and controlling its panel via aria-controls, so screen readers announce open/closed state and Enter/Space toggle from the platform

flexibility.note: type prop is free to choose based on whether sections are exclusive
Borders, surfaces, and radii must resolve to --cascade-* semantic tokens

components.name: Alert
components.description: Highlights a short, important message inline

whenToUse: Surfacing a persistent, contextual message inline within a view (warning, error, info, success)
Communicating status that should stay visible until read or resolved
Offering an inline recovery action tied to the message (action prop)
whenNotToUse: Transient confirmation that should auto-dismiss — use Toast
Blocking the user for a decision — use Modal or AlertDialog

antiPatterns.bad: Auto-dismissing an Alert on a timer to mimic a notification
antiPatterns.good: <Toast> for ephemeral feedback
antiPatterns.why: Alerts persist by design; auto-hiding important inline context risks the user missing it

related.name: Toast
related.reason: Toast auto-dismisses and floats; Alert persists inline in the layout

related.name: AlertDialog
related.reason: Use AlertDialog when the message must block and demand a choice

intent.a11yRationale: role="alert" makes assistive tech announce the message when it appears; the dismiss control is a real button so it is reachable and labeled, and color is paired with an icon/title so meaning is not conveyed by hue alone

content.tone: Direct and specific — state what happened and what to do next
content.notes: Title is a short summary; body explains. Avoid alarmist all-caps.

flexibility.note: Choose the severity variant that matches the message; default is neutral
Severity colors must resolve to --cascade-color-info/success/warning/destructive

components.name: AlertDialog
components.description: Confirmation dialog requiring explicit user action; no light-dismiss

whenToUse: Confirming a destructive or irreversible action (delete, overwrite, sign out) before it happens
Interrupting a flow to force an explicit decision the user must acknowledge
whenNotToUse: Showing non-critical information or forms — use Modal, which supports light-dismiss and arbitrary content
Confirming a low-stakes action where an undoable Toast is friendlier and less interruptive

antiPatterns.bad: Using AlertDialog for a multi-field form or rich content
antiPatterns.good: Use Modal for content; reserve AlertDialog for a title + description + confirm/cancel decision
antiPatterns.why: AlertDialog only renders a title, description, and two action buttons, and is role="alertdialog" with no light-dismiss — it is built to demand a yes/no answer, not host content

antiPatterns.bad: Adding a backdrop/Escape close to dismiss the dialog without choosing
antiPatterns.why: It uses popover="manual" so it cannot be light-dismissed by design — closing must go through onConfirm/onCancel so the decision is always explicit

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Use for general dialogs with content and light-dismiss; AlertDialog is the must-acknowledge confirm

related.name: Toast
related.reason: Prefer an undoable Toast for reversible low-stakes actions instead of a blocking confirm

related.name: Button
related.reason: A Button typically triggers the action that opens the AlertDialog

intent.a11yRationale: Uses role="alertdialog" with aria-modal and labelled/described-by wiring so assistive tech announces it as an interruptive decision; focus moves to the cancel button on open so the safe default is selected, and popover="manual" prevents accidental dismissal that would skip the decision

content.tone: Direct and specific — name the exact consequence
content.notes: Title states the action ("Delete project?"); description states what is lost and whether it is reversible; confirm label is the verb ("Delete"), not "OK"

flexibility.note: No light-dismiss — closing must go through onConfirm/onCancel
confirm/cancel copy overridable via labels; defaults come from the i18n catalog
Visual styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* tokens; destructive variant uses --cascade-color-destructive

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Displays a user image with initials fallback

whenToUse: Representing a person or entity with a thumbnail image and initials fallback
Showing presence/status alongside an identity (status prop)
Compactly identifying authors in lists, comments, and headers
whenNotToUse: Decorative imagery unrelated to identity — use a plain
A generic icon or logo with no person/entity meaning

antiPatterns.bad: <Avatar src="/logo.png" /> with no alt and no fallback
antiPatterns.good: <Avatar src="/jane.jpg" alt="Jane Doe" fallback="JD" />
antiPatterns.why: Without alt/fallback the identity is invisible to assistive tech and breaks on image load failure

related.name: Status
related.reason: Use Status for a standalone state dot not attached to an identity

intent.a11yRationale: role="img" with alt names the person; on image error it falls back to initials so the identity survives; the status dot carries an accessible label rather than relying on color alone

flexibility.note: Pick the size that fits the surrounding density
Fallback and status colors must resolve to --cascade-* semantic tokens

components.name: Badge
components.description: Small status label or category indicator

whenToUse: Labeling an item with a short, static status or category (e.g. "New", "Beta")
Annotating an element with a count or state that is not interactive
whenNotToUse: A removable or interactive chip (filters, selections) — use Tag
A standalone system state with a colored dot — use Status

antiPatterns.bad: <Badge onClick={removeFilter}>Active</Badge>
antiPatterns.good: <Tag onDismiss={removeFilter}>Active</Tag>
antiPatterns.why: Badge is a non-interactive label; interactive/removable labels belong to Tag with proper button semantics

related.name: Tag
related.reason: Tag is the interactive, dismissible counterpart

related.name: Status
related.reason: Status pairs a colored dot with a label for live system state

intent.a11yRationale: role="status" lets assistive tech expose the label as state; meaning is reinforced by text, never by color alone

flexibility.note: Choose the variant that matches the semantic meaning
Colors and radius must resolve to --cascade-* tokens (--cascade-radius-badge)

components.name: Blockquote
components.description: Quoted passage with optional attribution footer

whenToUse: Setting off a quoted passage from another source with visual emphasis
Attributing a quote to a person or work via the cite footer
whenNotToUse: Indenting or emphasizing your own non-quoted text — use Text styling
Inline quotations within a sentence — use a <q> element

antiPatterns.bad: Using <Blockquote> purely to indent a callout
antiPatterns.good: A Card or styled Text block for non-quote emphasis
antiPatterns.why: blockquote semantics tell assistive tech the content is a quotation; misusing it misleads readers

related.name: Prose
related.reason: Prose styles blockquotes among other long-form elements automatically

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <blockquote> with the attribution in <footer><cite>, so the quotation and its source are semantically distinct to assistive tech

content.tone: Verbatim quotation; do not paraphrase inside the quote
content.notes: cite names the author or work, not a URL label

flexibility.note: cite is optional; omit when the source is given in surrounding context
Border and text colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.description: Shows the current page location within a navigation hierarchy

whenToUse: Showing the user where the current page sits in a navigation hierarchy
Providing one-click navigation back to ancestor pages
Collapsing deep trails to keep the trail compact (maxVisible)
whenNotToUse: Paging through data records — use Pagination
Switching between sibling views — use Tabs

antiPatterns.bad: Using Breadcrumb to step through pages 1, 2, 3 of a dataset
antiPatterns.good: <Pagination> for paged data
antiPatterns.why: Breadcrumb models hierarchy depth, not sequential position in a list

related.name: Pagination
related.reason: Pagination moves through data pages; Breadcrumb moves up a hierarchy

intent.a11yRationale: Wrapped in <nav> with an aria-label and the current page marked aria-current="page"; ancestor entries are real links so keyboard users can jump up the hierarchy

content.tone: Short labels matching page titles
content.notes: The last item is the current page and is not a link

flexibility.note: Collapse long trails to fit available width
Text colors and focus ring must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Button
components.description: Triggers an action or event

whenToUse: Triggering an action or navigation the user initiates by click/press
Submitting a form or confirming a decision
whenNotToUse: Navigation between pages where a real link is semantically correct — use an anchor
Toggling a binary setting — use Toggle; persistent selection — use Checkbox/Radio

antiPatterns.bad: <Button onClick={() => navigate("/x")}>Home</Button>
antiPatterns.good: Home
antiPatterns.why: Buttons are for actions, links for navigation — assistive tech and the browser treat them differently

related.name: Toggle
related.reason: Use for binary on/off state, not one-shot actions

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: A button often triggers a menu

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <button> so Enter/Space activation, focus, and role come from the platform; loading state uses aria-busy rather than removing the element so focus is preserved

content.tone: Imperative verb-first label
content.notes: Sentence case, ≤3 words; avoid "Click here"

flexibility.note: Visual props must resolve to --cascade-button-* / semantic tokens
Free, within tone guidance

components.name: Card
components.description: Container for grouping related content

whenToUse: Grouping related content into a visually distinct surface with border/shadow
Creating scannable units in a grid or list (dashboard tiles, item summaries)
Giving a content cluster elevation to separate it from the page background
whenNotToUse: Pure semantic/structural grouping with no surface — use a <section>
Wrapping every element in a card — nesting surfaces flattens visual hierarchy

antiPatterns.bad: Nesting Cards several levels deep for layout
antiPatterns.good: A single Card with internal spacing, or a plain <section>
antiPatterns.why: Stacked surfaces and shadows compete for attention and muddy the hierarchy

related.name: Separator
related.reason: Use a Separator to divide regions inside a card

intent.a11yRationale: role="region" is appropriate only when the card is a meaningful landmark; otherwise treat it as presentational — the visual surface adds no semantics on its own

flexibility.note: Choose elevation and density to fit the surrounding layout
Surface, border, radius, and shadow must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Binary toggle for forms, with indeterminate support

whenToUse: Selecting zero or more options from a list where each choice is independent
A single opt-in within a form (accept terms, subscribe) submitted alongside other fields
A "select all" control that needs an indeterminate (partial) state
whenNotToUse: A binary setting that applies immediately on change — use Toggle
Choosing exactly one option from a mutually exclusive set — use Radio

antiPatterns.bad: <Checkbox label="Dark mode" /> that flips a setting live
antiPatterns.good: <Toggle label="Dark mode" />
antiPatterns.why: Checkbox signals a form value to be submitted; Toggle signals an immediate on/off state change

antiPatterns.bad: Two checkboxes for one either/or choice
antiPatterns.good: Use Radio for mutually exclusive options
antiPatterns.why: Independent checkboxes let the user select both or neither, which a single exclusive choice should not allow

related.name: Toggle
related.reason: Use for immediate binary settings rather than form values

related.name: Radio
related.reason: Use for selecting exactly one from a mutually exclusive set

related.name: CheckboxCard
related.reason: Use the card variant when each option needs a title plus description

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <input type="checkbox"> wrapped in a <label>, so role, checked/indeterminate state, Space activation, and label association come from the platform; the indeterminate visual is set on the DOM node since it is a property not an attribute

flexibility.note: Spreads InputHTMLAttributes — name, value, required, checked, onChange all pass through
Control styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-sm tokens

components.name: CheckboxCard
components.description: Multi-selectable card backed by a native checkbox. Use multiple independent CheckboxCards for multi-select scenarios.

whenToUse: Multi-select where each option needs a title and supporting description (feature toggles, add-ons)
Presenting selectable options as larger, scannable click targets rather than a dense checkbox list
whenNotToUse: A simple labelled boolean in a form — use Checkbox
Choosing exactly one option from a set — use RadioCard or Radio

antiPatterns.bad: Using CheckboxCard for mutually exclusive options
antiPatterns.good: Use RadioCard so only one can be selected
antiPatterns.why: Each CheckboxCard is an independent native checkbox — nothing enforces a single selection across them

antiPatterns.bad: Cramming a paragraph of marketing copy into description
antiPatterns.why: The description is a single supporting line; long content breaks the card layout and scannability

related.name: Checkbox
related.reason: Use the plain checkbox when a title/description card is overkill

related.name: RadioCard
related.reason: Use for single-select card groups

intent.a11yRationale: Backed by a native <input type="checkbox"> inside a <label>, so the entire card is clickable and role, checked state, Space activation, and label association come from the platform; the SVG glyph is aria-hidden as it is purely decorative

content.tone: Title is a concise noun phrase; description is one supporting line
content.notes: Keep description to a single line of context (what it does, plan requirements)

flexibility.note: Supports both checked + onCheckedChange and defaultChecked
Card styling resolves to --cascade-color-accent / --cascade-color-border / --cascade-radius-surface

components.name: Code
components.description: Inline code span for identifiers, commands, and short snippets

whenToUse: Marking up an inline identifier, command, path, or token within prose
Distinguishing literal code from surrounding text with a monospace span
whenNotToUse: Multi-line code blocks with syntax highlighting — use a <pre> block
Keyboard shortcuts the user should press — use Kbd

antiPatterns.bad: Press Cmd+K
antiPatterns.good: <Kbd>⌘</Kbd> <Kbd>K</Kbd>
antiPatterns.why: Code marks literal code; key presses are semantically Kbd

related.name: Kbd
related.reason: Kbd is for keys to press, not code to read

related.name: Prose
related.reason: Prose styles inline <code> automatically in authored content

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <code> element so assistive tech can expose the content as code; relies on monospace and surface, not color alone, to distinguish it

flexibility.note: sm fits dense UI; md matches body text
Font and surface must resolve to --cascade-font-mono and --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Combobox
components.description: Filterable single-select with an animated custom listbox, built on the dropdown open/close machine

whenToUse: Single-select from a long list where type-to-filter makes finding an option faster
Form fields where the value is one of many known options (country, assignee, repository)
whenNotToUse: Short option lists (≈2–7) where filtering adds no value — use Select or SegmentedControl
Selecting multiple values — use MultiSelect
Triggering actions or commands — use Dropdown or CommandMenu

antiPatterns.bad: Using Combobox to fire actions like "Delete" or "Export"
antiPatterns.good: Use Dropdown for actions; Combobox is for picking a value
antiPatterns.why: Combobox has role="combobox" with a listbox of selectable values and onChange semantics — actions belong in a menu

antiPatterns.bad: Setting searchable={false} on a 200-item list
antiPatterns.why: Without filtering the list becomes an unusable scroll; either keep search on or switch to a different selection pattern

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use for short lists that do not need type-to-filter

related.name: MultiSelect
related.reason: Use when more than one value can be selected

related.name: CommandMenu
related.reason: Use the Cmd+K palette for command/navigation search rather than value selection

intent.a11yRationale: Trigger exposes role="combobox" with aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-haspopup="listbox", and aria-activedescendant pointing at the active option so screen readers track focus through arrow navigation without moving DOM focus off the input; error state is wired via aria-invalid + aria-describedby and announced with role="alert"

flexibility.note: Filtering can be toggled off for short lists via searchable={false}
Supports value + onChange or defaultValue
Listbox/field styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-* tokens

components.name: CommandMenu
components.description: Cmd+K command palette with fuzzy search over grouped commands

whenToUse: Giving power users a global Cmd/Ctrl+K palette to search and run actions or navigate
Exposing many commands across grouped categories that benefit from fuzzy search
Multi-step flows where selecting an item drills into a nested page of further commands
whenNotToUse: Picking a form value from a list — use Combobox
A small set of actions tied to a specific trigger button — use Dropdown

antiPatterns.bad: Using CommandMenu as a form field to capture a selected value
antiPatterns.good: Use Combobox for value selection inside a form
antiPatterns.why: CommandMenu is a modal command palette whose items run onSelect callbacks or open sub-pages; it has no controlled form value

antiPatterns.bad: Items with both onSelect and page set, or neither
antiPatterns.why: Each item must do exactly one thing — run an action (onSelect) or push a nested page (page)

related.name: Combobox
related.reason: Use for filtering and selecting a form value rather than running commands

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: Use for a small action menu anchored to a button instead of a global palette

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Renders inside a native modal dialog with backdrop dismiss

intent.a11yRationale: The search input is role="combobox" with aria-controls, aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete="list" and aria-activedescendant so navigation is announced without moving DOM focus from the input; it renders in a native <dialog> for focus trapping, the empty/loading state uses role="status", and footer hints are aria-hidden as redundant decoration

content.tone: Action-oriented item labels; short group headings
content.notes: Item labels are verb-first commands; use keywords for synonyms users might type

flexibility.note: Global Cmd/Ctrl+K binding can be disabled via hotkey={false} for custom triggering
onQueryChange + loading allow fetching items per keystroke
Palette styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-radius-modal tokens

components.name: ContextMenu
components.description: Right-click context menu anchored at pointer coordinates via CSS custom properties

whenToUse: Offering actions contextual to an element via right-click (rename, delete, copy on a row or canvas item)
Power-user surfaces where the desktop right-click affordance is expected
whenNotToUse: Primary actions that must be discoverable by all users — right-click is hidden; use a visible Button or Dropdown
Selecting a value or filtering a list — use Combobox/Select

antiPatterns.bad: Putting the only path to an action behind a right-click ContextMenu
antiPatterns.good: Also expose it via a visible Dropdown or Button; treat the context menu as a shortcut
antiPatterns.why: Right-click is undiscoverable and unavailable on touch, so critical actions become unreachable for many users

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: Use a button-triggered menu when actions must be visible and touch-accessible

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Use a general menu when the trigger is not a right-click gesture

intent.a11yRationale: The menu container is role="menu" and items are role="menuitem" with tabIndex management and aria-disabled, and it uses popover="auto" so it light-dismisses on Escape or outside click; the toggle event syncs that dismissal back into state so focus and open state stay consistent

flexibility.note: Accepts arbitrary ContextMenuItem children after the trigger child
Anchored at pointer coordinates via --cascade-context-x/y custom properties set on right-click

components.name: CopyButton
components.description: Icon button that copies a value to the clipboard with copied feedback

whenToUse: Letting users copy a short value to the clipboard — install commands, API keys, tokens, share links
Inline next to code snippets or read-only fields where copying is the primary affordance
whenNotToUse: Triggering a general action — use Button
Copying content the user can already select and edit freely where a copy affordance adds nothing

antiPatterns.bad: Wiring CopyButton to perform a non-clipboard action via onClick
antiPatterns.good: Use Button for arbitrary actions; CopyButton always writes value to the clipboard
antiPatterns.why: Its click handler is fixed to navigator.clipboard.writeText(value) plus copied feedback — overloading it breaks user expectation

related.name: Button
related.reason: Use for actions other than copying to clipboard

related.name: Input
related.reason: Often placed alongside a read-only field whose value it copies

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <button> so Enter/Space, focus, and role come from the platform; the aria-label swaps between copy and copied labels to announce the state change to screen readers since the icon swap alone is silent, and the SVG icons are aria-hidden

content.tone: Concise action/confirmation labels
content.notes: Default copy/copied strings come from the i18n catalog; override per instance via labels (e.g. "Copy token" / "Token copied")

flexibility.note: copy/copied strings overridable via labels; default from i18n catalog
Styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* tokens; copied state uses --cascade-color-success

components.name: DataTable
components.description: Signal-driven data table with client/server sort, filter, pagination, multi-selection, expandable rows, and CSS content-visibility row containment for large datasets

whenToUse: Displaying tabular data with columns the user sorts, filters, or pages through
Selecting rows for batch actions across a dataset
Rendering large datasets that benefit from row containment and server-side sort/paging
whenNotToUse: A simple static list of items — use List
Layout grids of cards or media — use a Card grid, not a data table

antiPatterns.bad: Using DataTable for two columns of label/value pairs
antiPatterns.good: A description list or a small Card with Stat/Text
antiPatterns.why: The full table machinery (sort, paging, selection) is overhead when there is no dataset to operate on

related.name: Pagination
related.reason: DataTable embeds pagination controls for paged data

related.name: EmptyState
related.reason: Render an EmptyState via the emptyState prop when there are no rows

intent.a11yRationale: Built on a native <table> with proper header semantics; sortable headers expose sort state, selection uses real checkboxes, and arrow-key navigation follows the grid pattern so keyboard users can traverse cells

flexibility.note: Choose client/server sort and density to fit data size and layout
All surfaces, borders, and spacing must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: DatePicker
components.description: An accessible date-picker with a calendar popover.

whenToUse: Picking a single calendar date in a form where a visual month grid helps (due dates, bookings)
Date entry that benefits from min/max constraints and locale-aware formatting and week start
whenNotToUse: Selecting a time of day — use TimePicker
Free-form or approximate dates where a plain Input is faster, or a date already known by typing

antiPatterns.bad: Passing a localized display string as value
antiPatterns.good: value/defaultValue/min/max are ISO YYYY-MM-DD; display formatting is handled internally
antiPatterns.why: The component parses and compares ISO dates; non-ISO values break selection, constraints, and onChange

related.name: TimePicker
related.reason: Combine when both a date and a time are needed

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use a plain input when a calendar grid is unnecessary

related.name: Form
related.reason: Typically a field within a form with label/hint/error

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger is role="combobox" with aria-haspopup="dialog"/aria-expanded; the calendar is a role="dialog" containing a role="grid" with roving tabindex so arrow keys move a single focusable day, days expose aria-pressed for selection, aria-current="date" for today, and aria-disabled past min/max, and errors are wired via aria-invalid + aria-describedby with role="alert"

flexibility.note: All date props are ISO YYYY-MM-DD strings
Display, weekday labels, and week start derive from the current i18n locale via Intl
Styling resolves to --cascade-date-picker-* component tokens

components.name: Dropdown
components.description: Menu of actions revealed from a trigger

whenToUse: Revealing a short menu of actions from a trigger button (row actions, "More" overflow, account menu)
Grouping related commands behind one affordance with optional icons and separators
whenNotToUse: Selecting a persistent value for a form — use Select or Combobox
A global searchable command palette — use CommandMenu; right-click context actions — use ContextMenu

antiPatterns.bad: Using Dropdown to choose a form value and reading the selection as field state
antiPatterns.good: Use Select/Combobox which expose role="combobox" and a controlled value
antiPatterns.why: Dropdown items are role="menuitem" actions fired via onSelect — there is no selected-value semantics for assistive tech or forms

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use for choosing and persisting a single form value

related.name: CommandMenu
related.reason: Use for a searchable global action palette

related.name: Button
related.reason: The trigger is typically a Button passed via the trigger prop

intent.a11yRationale: The menu is role="menu" with role="menuitem" buttons and roving tabindex; the trigger gets aria-haspopup="menu" and aria-expanded via cloneElement; arrow/Home/End/Enter/Escape are handled and focus returns to the trigger on select or close, and popover="auto" provides light-dismiss synced back to state via the toggle event

flexibility.note: Any ReactElement works as the trigger; ref and aria props are merged in
Supports open + onOpenChange or fully internal state
Menu styling resolves to semantic --cascade-color-* / --cascade-z-dropdown tokens

components.name: Editable
components.description: Inline click-to-edit text field

whenToUse: Editing a single piece of text in place where the value is normally read-only — titles, names, labels
Keeping the page layout stable: the preview and the edit input occupy the same spot
Letting users opt into editing by clicking, without a separate edit mode or form
whenNotToUse: A field that is always meant to be edited — use Input inside a Form instead
Multi-line or rich text — this is a single-line input only
Collecting several related values at once — use a Form with grouped fields

antiPatterns.bad: <Editable value={email} onValueChange={save} /> as a primary form control
antiPatterns.good: <Input label="Email" value={email} onChange={...} />
antiPatterns.why: Inline edit hides the affordance behind a click; required form fields should be visibly editable from the start

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use Input when the field is always editable rather than click-to-reveal

intent.a11yRationale: The preview renders as a real <button> so it is keyboard-focusable and announces as an actionable control; while editing, Enter confirms and Escape cancels, and focus is moved into the input and its text selected so keyboard users can immediately type.

content.tone: Placeholder describes the value, not the action
content.notes: Sentence case; e.g. "Enter title" not "Click to edit"

flexibility.note: Visual styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* surface/border/accent/text tokens
Free, within tone guidance
submitOnBlur toggles whether blurring confirms or cancels the edit

components.name: EmptyState
components.description: Placeholder for views that have no data to display

whenToUse: Filling a view that has no data with guidance and a primary next step
Explaining why a list/table is empty (no results, nothing created yet)
Offering an action that resolves the empty condition (action prop)
whenNotToUse: Content is still loading — use Skeleton or Spinner
An error occurred — use Alert to explain the failure

antiPatterns.bad: Showing an EmptyState while data is still fetching
antiPatterns.good: A Skeleton during load, then EmptyState only if the result is empty
antiPatterns.why: Conflating loading with empty tells the user there is no data when there may be

related.name: Skeleton
related.reason: Skeleton covers the loading phase before data resolves

related.name: Alert
related.reason: Use Alert for error states rather than a generic empty state

intent.a11yRationale: Presentational by role; the title carries the meaning as text and the action is a real focusable control, so keyboard and screen-reader users can act on the next step

content.tone: Encouraging and concrete — name the next action
content.notes: Title states the situation; description tells the user what to do

flexibility.note: Action and icon are optional; size scales for full-page vs in-panel use
Text and background colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: FileUploader
components.description: Drag-and-drop file upload zone with file list and status indicators.

whenToUse: Letting users attach one or more files via click-to-browse or drag-and-drop
Showing per-file upload progress and status (uploading, complete, error) in a list
Enforcing client-side accept/maxSize constraints with a callback for rejected files
whenNotToUse: A single short text value — use Input
Choosing from a fixed set of options rather than supplying a file — use Select or MultiSelect

antiPatterns.bad: Treating the component as uncontrolled and ignoring onFilesAdded
antiPatterns.good: Track accepted files in state and pass them back via the files prop
antiPatterns.why: The file list is controlled — without wiring files/onFilesAdded/onRemove nothing renders in the list and status cannot update

antiPatterns.bad: Relying only on accept/maxSize for security
antiPatterns.good: Re-validate type and size on the server after upload
antiPatterns.why: Client-side accept/maxSize are UX filters, not enforcement; a determined user can bypass them

related.name: Form
related.reason: Place the uploader inside a Form when files are part of a larger submission

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use a plain text Input when capturing a value rather than a file

intent.a11yRationale: The drop zone is a real <button> so it is keyboard-operable (Enter/Space open the native file picker), the hidden file input is removed from the tab order and labelled via aria-describedby, and the file list uses aria-live="polite" so status changes are announced; each remove control carries an aria-label naming its file.

content.tone: Plain, instructive labels and hints
content.notes: Defaults come from the i18n catalog; hint should state accepted types/size limits, e.g. "PDF up to 5 MB"

flexibility.note: Status colors must resolve to --cascade-color-accent/success/danger tokens
Overridable via label/hint props or the labels object
Configure freely; rejected files surface through onRejected

components.name: Form
components.description: Typed signal-based form store (createForm/useForm) with sync/async validation and a thin Form element wrapper

whenToUse: Collecting and submitting a set of related field values together
Running sync or async validation (including Standard Schema like zod/valibot/arktype) before invoking onValid
Tracking per-field touched state and errors via the signal-based store from useForm/createForm
whenNotToUse: A single standalone value with no submission step — render a bare Input
Inline editing of one read-only value — use Editable

antiPatterns.bad: <form onSubmit={...}> with hand-rolled useState per field
antiPatterns.good: const form = useForm({ initialValues, validate }); <Form form={form} onValid={...}>
antiPatterns.why: The store centralizes values, errors, touched, and submitting as signals; rolling your own reintroduces the re-render and validation-timing bugs the store solves

related.name: Input
related.reason: Form wraps field controls like Input, wiring value/onChange/onBlur/error from form.field()

related.name: FileUploader
related.reason: File attachments can participate in a Form submission

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <form> with noValidate so validation messages come from the component (surfaced per field via Input error/role="alert") rather than inconsistent browser bubbles, while Enter-to-submit and the form role are preserved by the platform element.

flexibility.note: Use schema, validate, or both; schema runs first and validate only if the schema passes
Any control can be wired via form.field(name); children are free-form
onValid only fires when validation produces no errors; submission is guarded by the submitting signal

components.name: Header
components.description: App top bar with brand, primary navigation links, and an actions slot

whenToUse: Providing a simple marketing or app top bar with brand, links, and actions
Anchoring primary navigation across pages of a site
Keeping the bar visible while scrolling (sticky)
whenNotToUse: Dense console apps with dropdown nav, global icon actions, and a hamburger — use ShellHeader
Section headings within page content — use Heading

antiPatterns.bad: Building complex multi-menu console chrome on top of Header
antiPatterns.good: <ShellHeader> for console-grade navigation
antiPatterns.why: Header is intentionally simple; console patterns belong to ShellHeader which provides them natively

related.name: ShellHeader
related.reason: ShellHeader is the console-grade header with dropdowns and icon actions

intent.a11yRationale: role="banner" marks the page header landmark; the active link is marked aria-current="page", and links/actions are real focusable controls so keyboard users can navigate the bar

flexibility.note: Brand, links, and actions slots are optional and composable
Surface, border, and z-index must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: HeaderPanel
components.description: Non-modal panel anchored below the shell header at the inline-end edge — hosts notifications, app switcher, user settings

whenToUse: Hosting a non-modal panel anchored under the shell header (notifications, switcher, settings)
Showing supplementary content triggered by a ShellHeader icon action
Light-dismiss content that should not block the rest of the app
whenNotToUse: Content that must block interaction until resolved — use Modal
A small contextual menu attached to a trigger — use Dropdown or Popover

antiPatterns.bad: Using HeaderPanel for a blocking confirmation
antiPatterns.good: <Modal> or <AlertDialog> for decisions that must block
antiPatterns.why: HeaderPanel is non-modal and light-dismissable; blocking flows need modal semantics

related.name: ShellHeader
related.reason: Opened by a ShellHeader action whose active state mirrors the panel open state

related.name: Switcher
related.reason: A Switcher commonly lives inside a HeaderPanel

intent.a11yRationale: role="region" with the label prop names the panel as a landmark; Escape closes it and focus is managed so keyboard users can dismiss it without a mouse

flexibility.note: Children are arbitrary panel content
Surface, shadow, and sizing must resolve to --cascade-* shell tokens

components.name: Heading
components.description: Section heading with semantic level decoupled from visual size

whenToUse: Titling a page or section as part of the document outline
Keeping the semantic heading level correct while choosing the visual size independently
whenNotToUse: Emphasizing inline or body text — use Text with a weight
Long-form rendered content — let Prose style its own headings

antiPatterns.bad: <Heading level={4} size="2xl"> chosen only to look big, skipping h2/h3
antiPatterns.good: Keep levels sequential; use size to control scale within the correct level
antiPatterns.why: Skipping heading levels breaks the document outline screen-reader users rely on to navigate

related.name: Text
related.reason: Text is for body copy; Heading is for outline structure

related.name: Prose
related.reason: Prose styles headings inside authored long-form content

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a real <h1>–<h6> from the level prop so the heading appears in the accessibility tree at the correct level; visual size is decoupled so styling never forces an incorrect level

content.tone: Concise, descriptive titles in sentence case
content.notes: Each page should have one h1

flexibility.note: size may override the level-derived default for visual scale
Levels must not skip — maintain a valid heading hierarchy

components.name: HoverCard
components.description: Hover-triggered popover with configurable open/close delay

whenToUse: Showing a rich preview (avatar, summary, links) when a user hovers or focuses a trigger
Supplementary, non-essential context the user can ignore without losing functionality
Cases needing an open/close delay so brief mouse passes do not flicker the card
whenNotToUse: Short plain-text hints — use Tooltip
Content the user must interact with or that should trap focus — use Popover or Modal
Touch-primary flows where there is no hover — prefer a tap-triggered Popover

antiPatterns.bad: Putting required actions (buttons users must click) inside HoverCardContent
antiPatterns.good: Use a Popover triggered by click so the surface is reliably reachable
antiPatterns.why: Hover surfaces are transient and unreachable by touch and many keyboard paths, so essential controls get lost

related.name: Tooltip
related.reason: Use Tooltip for brief text labels rather than rich preview content

related.name: Popover
related.reason: Use Popover for click-triggered, interactive, focus-managed content

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger opens on both mouseenter and focus (and closes on mouseleave/blur) so keyboard users get the same preview, and the content is marked role="complementary" to signal it is supplementary rather than a required dialog; the open/close delays prevent accidental flicker.

flexibility.note: Surface, border, radius, shadow, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
openDelay and closeDelay are tunable per instance (defaults 300/100ms)
HoverCardContent accepts arbitrary children

components.name: Input
components.description: Text input field with optional label, hint, and error state

whenToUse: Collecting a single line of free-form text from the user
Pairing a labelled field with optional hint and validation error messaging
As a field control inside a Form, wired via form.field()
whenNotToUse: Multi-line text — use Textarea
Choosing from a fixed list of options — use Select, Combobox, or MultiSelect
Editing one read-only value in place — use Editable

antiPatterns.bad: <Input placeholder="Email" /> with no label
antiPatterns.good: <Input label="Email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
antiPatterns.why: Placeholder text disappears on input and is not a substitute for a persistent, programmatically associated label

related.name: Form
related.reason: Input is the primary field control wired into a Form store

related.name: InputGroup
related.reason: Wrap Input in InputGroup to add prefix/suffix addons

related.name: Textarea
related.reason: Use Textarea for multi-line input

intent.a11yRationale: The label is associated to the input via htmlFor/id, error text is linked through aria-describedby and announced with role="alert", and aria-invalid is set when an error is present so assistive tech reports the field as erroneous; visual focus state is driven by CSS, not tracked imperatively.

content.tone: Concise noun label naming the value
content.notes: Sentence case label; hint and error are full sentences; error states what is wrong and how to fix it

flexibility.note: Surface, border, accent, destructive, radius, and focus-ring must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
Free, within content tone guidance
sm | md | lg, defaulting to md

components.name: InputGroup
components.description: Prefix/suffix addon wrapper for Input; InputGroupAddon renders inline icons/units inside the field border; ButtonGroup collapses adjacent button borders

whenToUse: Attaching a prefix/suffix addon (protocol, currency, unit) to an Input so it reads as one field
Placing a leading or trailing inline icon/unit inside the field border via InputGroupAddon
Grouping adjacent buttons with collapsed shared borders via ButtonGroup
whenNotToUse: A standalone field with no adornment — use Input directly
Conveying meaning that the user must read via an addon — decorative addons are aria-hidden

antiPatterns.bad: <InputGroupAddon>Required</InputGroupAddon> to convey field status
antiPatterns.good: Use the Input error/hint props for meaningful text
antiPatterns.why: InputGroupAddon is aria-hidden and decorative, so screen readers never announce it

related.name: Input
related.reason: InputGroup composes around an Input to add prefix/suffix addons

related.name: Button
related.reason: ButtonGroup arranges adjacent Buttons with merged borders

intent.a11yRationale: Inline addons are marked aria-hidden because they are purely decorative units/icons, so the wrapped Input keeps its own accessible name; ButtonGroup uses role="group" to convey that its buttons form a related set.

flexibility.note: Addon background, border, text, and radius must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
InputGroupAddon align is inline-start (leading) or inline-end (trailing)
prefix/suffix and addon children accept arbitrary ReactNode

components.name: Kbd
components.description: Displays a keyboard key or shortcut

whenToUse: Showing a keyboard key or shortcut the user should press
Documenting hotkeys in menus, tooltips, or help text
whenNotToUse: Inline code, commands, or identifiers — use Code
A short label that is not a key press — use Badge or Text

antiPatterns.bad: <Kbd>npm install</Kbd>
antiPatterns.good: npm install
antiPatterns.why: Kbd is for keys to press, not shell commands or code

related.name: Code
related.reason: Code marks literal code; Kbd marks keys to press

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <kbd> element so assistive tech identifies the content as keyboard input; compose multiple <kbd> for a chord rather than encoding the combination in one string

flexibility.note: sm fits inline help; md matches body text
Surface and border must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Link
components.description: Styled anchor for navigation, standalone or inline within prose

whenToUse: Navigating to another page, view, or resource via a real href
Inline cross-references within prose (variant="inline")
Linking to an external destination with a clear new-tab indicator (external)
whenNotToUse: Triggering an action or mutation with no destination — use Button
Submitting a form — use a submit Button

antiPatterns.bad: <Link onClick={doThing}> with no href
antiPatterns.good: <Button onClick={doThing}>
antiPatterns.why: Links are for navigation; actions belong to buttons so keyboard and assistive tech behave correctly

related.name: Button
related.reason: Button is for actions; Link is for navigation

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <a> so role, Enter activation, and focus come from the platform; external links add rel="noreferrer" and a visual indicator so users know a new tab will open

content.tone: Descriptive link text that makes sense out of context
content.notes: Avoid "click here"; the text should name the destination

flexibility.note: standalone vs inline depending on whether the link sits in prose
Accent colors and focus ring must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: List
components.description: Styled unordered or ordered list with ListItem

whenToUse: Presenting a set of related items as a bulleted or numbered list
Conveying sequence with an ordered list (as="ol")
Keeping list semantics while hiding visual markers (marker="none")
whenNotToUse: Tabular data with multiple columns — use DataTable
Interactive navigation items — use SideNav or a menu

antiPatterns.bad: A stack of <Text> rows used as a visual list
antiPatterns.good: <List><ListItem>…</ListItem></List>
antiPatterns.why: Real list markup lets screen readers announce item count and position

related.name: Text
related.reason: ListItem content is typically body Text

intent.a11yRationale: Renders native <ul>/<ol> with <li> children so assistive tech announces the list and its length; marker="none" hides bullets visually without removing list semantics

flexibility.note: disc/decimal/none chosen to match content and visual needs
Spacing and text color must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Menu
components.description: Dropdown menu with keyboard navigation, built on usePopover

whenToUse: Presenting a list of actions or commands triggered from a button
Action lists that need arrow-key navigation and Enter/Space activation
Grouping related commands with separators behind a single trigger
whenNotToUse: Selecting a persistent value from options — use Select or MultiSelect
Right-click contextual actions on an element — use ContextMenu
A single non-list action — use a Button

antiPatterns.bad: Using Menu items to pick a form value and showing the choice as selected
antiPatterns.good: Use Select for value selection; Menu items are one-shot actions
antiPatterns.why: Menu items use role="menuitem" and close on activation — they are not selectable options and do not model a chosen value

related.name: ContextMenu
related.reason: Use ContextMenu for right-click activation on a target element

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use Select when the user is choosing a value rather than firing an action

related.name: Button
related.reason: A button (MenuTrigger) opens the menu

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger exposes aria-haspopup="menu" and aria-expanded, the panel is role="menu" with role="menuitem" children, focus moves to the first enabled item on open, ArrowUp/ArrowDown rove focus between items, disabled items are aria-disabled and removed from the tab order, and separators use role="separator".

flexibility.note: Surface, border, radius, shadow, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
MenuItem accepts arbitrary children; onSelect defines the action
MenuItem and MenuSeparator can be mixed freely under the trigger

components.name: Modal
components.description: Accessible dialog overlay using native <dialog> element

whenToUse: Presenting focused content or a task that must interrupt the current flow
Confirmations and forms that require the user to act before continuing
Cases needing a modal backdrop, focus trap, and Escape-to-close from the native <dialog>
whenNotToUse: Anchored, non-modal content next to a trigger — use Popover
A side panel or drawer for secondary content — use Sheet
A destructive confirmation with a clear yes/no decision — use AlertDialog

antiPatterns.bad: Rendering <Modal> mounted but driving visibility with CSS display
antiPatterns.good: Control visibility with the open prop
antiPatterns.why: The open prop drives showModal()/close() on the native dialog, which manages the top layer, backdrop, and focus trap; hiding with CSS breaks all three

related.name: AlertDialog
related.reason: Use AlertDialog for a focused confirm/cancel decision, especially destructive ones

related.name: Sheet
related.reason: Use Sheet for an edge-anchored drawer rather than a centered dialog

related.name: Popover
related.reason: Use Popover for lightweight, non-modal content anchored to a trigger

intent.a11yRationale: Built on the native <dialog> element so showModal() provides a real focus trap, top-layer rendering, and inert background for free; role="dialog" plus aria-labelledby/aria-describedby tie the title and description to the dialog, and Escape closes via the platform.

content.tone: Clear title naming the task; concise body
content.notes: Title is a noun phrase; the close control label comes from the i18n catalog

flexibility.note: Overlay surface, border, radius, shadow, and focus-ring must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
sm | md | lg, defaulting to md
All optional; body accepts arbitrary children

components.name: MultiSelect
components.description: Searchable multi-value select with popover listbox

whenToUse: Selecting several values at once from a known list of options
Lists long enough that the built-in search/filter helps the user find options
Cases needing a compact trigger that summarizes the selected count
whenNotToUse: Choosing exactly one value — use Select
Allowing free-text entries not in a predefined list — use Combobox or TagsInput
A handful of always-visible options — use a Checkbox group

antiPatterns.bad: <MultiSelect value={value} onValueChange={...} /> without keeping value in sync
antiPatterns.good: Store the selected string[] and update it from onValueChange every toggle
antiPatterns.why: Selection is fully controlled; the listbox reflects value, so dropping the update leaves checkboxes out of sync with state

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use Select for single-value selection

related.name: Combobox
related.reason: Use Combobox when users may type values not in the list

related.name: Checkbox
related.reason: Use a Checkbox group for a small set of always-visible options

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger advertises aria-haspopup="listbox" and aria-expanded, the panel is role="listbox" with aria-multiselectable, each option is role="option" with aria-selected reflecting membership and aria-disabled for unavailable ones; ArrowUp/ArrowDown move the active option, Space/Enter toggle it, and Escape closes the popover.

content.tone: Plain placeholder and count summary
content.notes: Defaults from the i18n catalog; the selected-count and no-results strings are overridable via labels

flexibility.note: Surface, border, accent, radius, shadow, focus-ring, and motion must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
placeholder, selected(count), search, and noResults are overridable
Caller supplies the option list and may mark individual options disabled

components.name: NumberInput
components.description: Numeric input with stepper buttons, clamping, precision, and locale formatting

whenToUse: Collecting a bounded numeric value where increment/decrement by a known step is natural (quantity, opacity, count)
Numeric entry that needs clamping to min/max, fixed precision, or locale-aware display formatting (e.g. currency)
Inputs where keyboard ArrowUp/ArrowDown stepping speeds up adjustment
whenNotToUse: Free-form or non-numeric text — use Input
Choosing along a continuous range where the exact number matters less than the relative position — use Slider
Phone numbers, PINs, or codes that are digit strings rather than quantities — use Input or OtpInput

antiPatterns.bad: <Input type="number" onChange={...} />
antiPatterns.good: <NumberInput min={0} max={99} step={1} onChange={...} />
antiPatterns.why: NumberInput adds stepper buttons, clamping, precision rounding, and Intl formatting that a raw number input does not, and reports a parsed number | null rather than a string

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use for unbounded or non-numeric text entry

related.name: Slider
related.reason: Use when approximate position on a range matters more than an exact typed number

intent.a11yRationale: Exposes role="spinbutton" with aria-valuenow/min/max so assistive tech announces the current value and bounds; stepper buttons are taken out of the tab order (tabIndex=-1) and labeled, keeping keyboard focus on the field while ArrowUp/ArrowDown drive stepping.

flexibility.note: Visual props must resolve to the listed --cascade-* semantic tokens
min, max, step, precision, and formatOptions are free to suit the value domain
incrementLabel/decrementLabel override the i18n defaults

components.name: OtpInput
components.description: Segmented one-time code input

whenToUse: Entering a fixed-length one-time code (2FA, SMS verification, email confirmation) split across per-character slots
Codes that benefit from auto-advance between slots and pasting the full code at once
Numeric or alphanumeric short codes of a known length
whenNotToUse: Variable-length or free-form text — use Input
Secret credentials that should be masked — use PasswordInput
Long codes where per-character boxes add no value over a single field — use Input

antiPatterns.bad: <Input maxLength={6} placeholder="Enter code" />
antiPatterns.good: <OtpInput length={6} value={code} onValueChange={setCode} />
antiPatterns.why: OtpInput gives per-digit slots with auto-advance, backspace-to-previous, full-code paste handling, and autoComplete="one-time-code" that a single Input does not

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use for variable-length or non-code text entry

related.name: PasswordInput
related.reason: Use when the entered value is a secret that must be masked

intent.a11yRationale: Wraps the slots in role="group" with a localized aria-label and labels each slot with its position so screen readers announce which digit is being entered; the first slot carries autoComplete="one-time-code" so platform SMS autofill can populate the code.

flexibility.note: Slot styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
length and numeric/alphanumeric type are free to match the issued code format

components.name: OverflowMenu
components.description: Kebab icon button revealing a menu of row-level actions

whenToUse: Collapsing secondary row-level or item-level actions behind a kebab trigger in dense layouts like tables and lists
Offering a small set of actions where a destructive option needs visual distinction
whenNotToUse: A primary action that should always be visible — use Button
Selecting a single value from a set rather than triggering actions — use Select or Dropdown
New code — this component is deprecated in favor of Menu

antiPatterns.bad: Rendering every row action as a visible Button in a crowded table
antiPatterns.good: <OverflowMenu items={[{ label: "Edit", value: "edit" }, { label: "Delete", value: "delete", destructive: true }]} onSelect={handle} />
antiPatterns.why: A kebab menu keeps dense rows scannable; surfacing every action inline competes for attention and breaks alignment

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Preferred replacement; OverflowMenu is deprecated and delegates to Dropdown internally

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: OverflowMenu is a kebab-triggered wrapper around Dropdown

related.name: Button
related.reason: Use for a single always-visible action instead of hiding it in a menu

intent.a11yRationale: The kebab trigger carries a localized aria-label since it has no visible text, and the revealed list uses Dropdown's menu semantics so arrow keys, Home/End, Enter/Space, and Escape work without custom handling.

content.tone: Imperative verb-first action labels
content.notes: Sentence case, short; mark irreversible actions destructive

flexibility.note: Trigger and item styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
items, ariaLabel, and placement are free to suit the context

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Controls for navigating paged data sets, with page size selection

whenToUse: Navigating between pages of a paged dataset
Letting the user change how many items appear per page (onPageSizeChange)
Pairing with a table or list that loads data in chunks
whenNotToUse: Navigating a content hierarchy — use Breadcrumb
Infinite scroll experiences where pages are not exposed

antiPatterns.bad: Using Pagination to navigate between unrelated app sections
antiPatterns.good: <Tabs> or nav links for section switching
antiPatterns.why: Pagination semantics imply sequential pages of one dataset, not arbitrary navigation

related.name: DataTable
related.reason: DataTable embeds Pagination for its rows

related.name: Breadcrumb
related.reason: Breadcrumb is for hierarchy, Pagination for sequential pages

intent.a11yRationale: Wrapped in <nav> with an accessible label; page controls are real buttons with current-page state exposed, and all visible/assistive strings are overridable via labels for i18n

flexibility.note: pageSizeOptions and the size select are optional
Surfaces, borders, and accent must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: PasswordInput
components.description: Password input with reveal toggle and optional strength meter

whenToUse: Collecting a secret credential that should be masked by default with an opt-in reveal toggle
Password creation flows that benefit from inline strength feedback
whenNotToUse: Non-secret text — use Input
A fixed-length one-time verification code — use OtpInput

antiPatterns.bad: <Input type="password" />
antiPatterns.good: <PasswordInput showStrengthMeter />
antiPatterns.why: PasswordInput adds an accessible reveal/hide toggle and an optional strength meter on top of the masked field, which a raw password input lacks

related.name: Input
related.reason: Use for non-secret text that never needs masking

related.name: OtpInput
related.reason: Use for fixed-length one-time codes rather than passwords

intent.a11yRationale: The reveal control is a real <button> whose aria-label switches between reveal/hide so screen-reader users know the current masking state; toggling swaps the input type between password and text so the platform handles masking natively.

content.tone: Concise control labels
content.notes: reveal/hide and strength labels default from the i18n catalog; override via labels for custom copy or locale

flexibility.note: Field, toggle, and strength-meter styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
reveal, hide, and strengthLabel can be overridden via the labels prop

components.name: Popover
components.description: Anchored floating panel built on CSS Anchor Positioning + Popover API

whenToUse: Showing rich, interactive content anchored to a trigger that the user must explicitly open by clicking
Lightweight transient panels (forms, pickers, detail cards) that do not need to block the rest of the page
whenNotToUse: A short, non-interactive text hint on hover/focus — use Tooltip
A task that must capture focus and block interaction with the page — use Modal
Preview content revealed on hover without a click — use HoverCard

antiPatterns.bad: <Tooltip content={<form>...</form>}>
antiPatterns.good: <Popover><PopoverTrigger>…</PopoverTrigger><PopoverContent><form>…</form></PopoverContent></Popover>
antiPatterns.why: Tooltips are non-interactive and hover-driven; interactive content needs a click-opened dialog popover that users can move focus into

related.name: Tooltip
related.reason: Use for brief non-interactive hints, not interactive content

related.name: HoverCard
related.reason: Use when the panel should open on hover rather than click

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Use when the overlay must be blocking and focus-trapping

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger is a real <button> with aria-haspopup="dialog" and aria-expanded reflecting open state, and the panel uses role="dialog" via the native Popover API so Escape-to-close and light-dismiss come from the platform rather than custom handlers.

flexibility.note: Panel styling must resolve to the listed --cascade-* tokens
Trigger and panel content are arbitrary children supplied by the consumer

components.name: ProgressBar
components.description: Shows determinate or indeterminate progress of a task

whenToUse: Showing linear determinate progress of a task with a known percentage (value)
Indicating ongoing work of unknown duration (omit value for indeterminate)
Reflecting success/error outcome of a tracked operation (status)
whenNotToUse: A compact circular indicator is preferred — use ProgressCircle
Pure indeterminate spinning with no track — use Spinner
Stepping through a multi-step flow — use ProgressIndicator

antiPatterns.bad: A determinate ProgressBar with a faked, jumping value
antiPatterns.good: Omit value for an indeterminate bar when real progress is unknown
antiPatterns.why: A determinate bar implies a meaningful percentage; faking it misleads the user

related.name: ProgressCircle
related.reason: Circular form for compact determinate progress

related.name: Spinner
related.reason: Spinner for indeterminate work with no track

intent.a11yRationale: role="progressbar" with value/max exposes completion to assistive tech; the label is wired via aria-labelledby and success/error states add a glyph so outcome is not color-only

flexibility.note: Presence of value selects the mode
Fill and status colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: ProgressCircle
components.description: Circular determinate progress indicator rendered as an SVG arc

whenToUse: Showing determinate progress compactly as a circular arc
Displaying a percentage in a small footprint (showValue)
Tracking completion against a custom maximum (max)
whenNotToUse: Indeterminate work with no known value — use Spinner
Full-width linear progress with a label and helper text — use ProgressBar
Multi-step flows — use ProgressIndicator

antiPatterns.bad: Using ProgressCircle for indeterminate loading
antiPatterns.good: <Spinner> for indeterminate states
antiPatterns.why: ProgressCircle requires a determinate value; it cannot express unknown progress

related.name: ProgressBar
related.reason: Linear form with labels and indeterminate support

related.name: Spinner
related.reason: Indeterminate alternative when value is unknown

intent.a11yRationale: role="progressbar" with value/max conveys completion to assistive tech; the label prop supplies an accessible name since the SVG arc alone carries no text

flexibility.note: showValue pairs best with md/lg sizes
Arc and track colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: ProgressIndicator
components.description: Shows progress through the steps of a multi-step flow

whenToUse: Showing progress through the discrete steps of a multi-step flow (wizard, checkout)
Communicating which step is complete, current, and upcoming
Laying out steps horizontally or vertically with optional descriptions
whenNotToUse: Continuous task progress with a percentage — use ProgressBar or ProgressCircle
Indeterminate loading — use Spinner

antiPatterns.bad: Using ProgressIndicator to show a download percentage
antiPatterns.good: <ProgressBar value={pct}> for continuous progress
antiPatterns.why: ProgressIndicator models discrete steps, not a continuous quantity

related.name: ProgressBar
related.reason: ProgressBar shows continuous progress; this shows discrete steps

intent.a11yRationale: role="list" structures the steps in order; the current step is conveyed as text/state rather than color alone, so screen-reader users know where they are in the flow

content.tone: Short step labels; descriptions add detail
content.notes: Order matters — steps are read sequentially

flexibility.note: horizontal or vertical to fit the layout
Step and connector colors must resolve to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Prose
components.description: Wrapper that styles raw descendant HTML — headings, lists, code, quotes, tables

whenToUse: Styling long-form authored content (articles, docs, CMS output)
Applying readable typography to raw HTML you do not control (markdown pipelines)
Getting consistent spacing for headings, lists, code, and quotes in one wrapper
whenNotToUse: Single typographic elements in app UI — use Heading, Text, or List directly
Interactive component layouts — Prose only styles flow content

antiPatterns.bad: Wrapping app UI (buttons, forms) in <Prose> for spacing
antiPatterns.good: Use layout primitives; reserve Prose for authored document content
antiPatterns.why: Prose restyles all descendant HTML, which clobbers component styling and intent

related.name: Heading
related.reason: Use Heading/Text directly in app UI; Prose handles authored content

intent.a11yRationale: Adds no roles of its own — it styles descendant native elements, so the document semantics of the underlying HTML (headings, lists, code) are preserved for assistive tech

content.tone: Long-form editorial; the wrapper does not constrain voice
content.notes: Source HTML should be sanitized before dangerouslySetInnerHTML

flexibility.note: Accepts authored children or rendered markup
All typography and surface styling resolves to --cascade-* tokens

components.name: Radio
components.description: Single choice from a set, grouped with RadioGroup

whenToUse: Choosing exactly one option from a small, mutually exclusive set (roughly 2–6) where all options should stay visible
Form fields where the choices need plain text labels and a familiar radio affordance
whenNotToUse: Selecting one of many options where showing them all is impractical — use Select
Each option needs a title plus description or richer layout — use RadioCard
A compact inline switch between a few views or modes — use SegmentedControl

antiPatterns.bad: Multiple <Checkbox> where only one may be selected
